Purchasing and Canceling Offerings

After reading the detailed information for an offering, the player may decide to purchase it. When the player chooses to purchase an offering, the title calls the XOnlineOfferingPurchase function to perform the purchase operation. The XOnlineOfferingPurchase function should be called only for offerings that are not free, as determined by the fOfferingIsFree member of the XONLINE_PRICE structure.

XOnlineOfferingPurchase requires the following: 1) the user index of the currently logged-on user who will be making the purchase, and 2) the identifier that uniquely specifies the offering (the same identifier returned by the offering enumeration and used to retrieve the offering details). XOnlineOfferingPurchase creates an asynchronous online task and returns a code indicating the success or failure of the task creation. After a purchase operation has been initiated with XOnlineOfferingPurchase, it is not possible to halt the operation. Prior to task completion, some asynchronous online tasks can be halted by calling XOnlineTaskClose for the task handle. XOnlineOfferingPurchase, however, sends the purchase request to a server, and that request cannot be recalled. Always call XOnlineTaskContinue to completion for the purchase operation, and only then call XOnlineTaskClose to close the task handle.

If the offering purchased is cancelable (usually indicative of a subscription with a recurring charge), the user can later cancel his or her subscription by choosing to do so from within the game menus. If the user so chooses, the title calls XOnlineOfferingCancel to begin an asynchronous task that cancels the purchase. Like XOnlineOfferingPurchase, once the cancel operation has been initiated it cannot be halted by closing the task handle. Continue to call XOnlineTaskContinue until it returns a value other than XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING. Only after a value other than XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING is returned should XOnlineTaskClose be called to close the task handle for the cancel operation.

After an offering has been successfully purchased, and if it is associated with downloadable content, it must still be downloaded and installed to the local Xbox console in order for the player to make use of the offering.
